category_title: Elementary-aged children with mild/moderate autism and significant language delays
minimal_context: The student and a peer are playing with grocery store toys on the carpet in a sectioned-off hallway.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are going to play with the grocery store toys on the carpet for ten minutes. You and your partner can play together.

STUDENT: (Picks up a plastic apple) Red apple.

TUTOR: Your partner has the shopping basket.

STUDENT: (Looks at the basket) Basket.

TUTOR: What can you say to your partner?

STUDENT: (Reaches for the basket without asking) Put apple.

options.A: Use a visual chart for checking in during the play session, where specific numbers, colors, and faces related to anxiety are matched with feeling words.
options.B: Work on recognition of body language and facial cues with the peer while, at the same time, labeling feelings during the play session.
options.C: Develop a play plan specific to the grocery store theme: 1) identify the play theme; 2) have the children label the toys to be used; and 3) ask the children how they could play with the toys.
options.D: Have more new motivating grocery store toys handy during the 10-minute play session on the carpet in the hallway.
